summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the Execute Command node executes arbitrary shell commands without any validation, sanitization, or access controls, leading to complete system compromise.
expected_security_impact: Executing a workflow with the Execute Command node should result in arbitrary command execution on the host system. The proof of concept creates a file /tmp/pwned.txt containing evidence of command execution (user ID, username, and a marker string).
actual_observed_result: The Execute Command node successfully executed the command 'echo PWNED_BY_EVOUNT > /tmp/pwned.txt && id >> /tmp/pwned.txt && whoami >> /tmp/pwned.txt', creating /tmp/pwned.txt with the output. This demonstrates full command execution capability with the privileges of the n8n process.
verification_method: The reproduction creates a workflow with an Execute Command node that writes a proof file to /tmp/pwned.txt. After workflow execution, the verification script checks for the existence and content of this file. Successful file creation with expected content confirms unrestricted command execution.
evidence_produced_by_run: File /tmp/pwned.txt created by Execute Command node
File contains output of 'id' command showing user/group IDs of the n8n process
File contains output of 'whoami' command showing the username running n8n
Proof marker string 'PWNED_BY_EVOUNT' present in the file
No command validation, sanitization, or whitelisting was applied

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
